html:
Sarnafil: A PVC-First Manufacturer, Not a TPO Company With a PVC Line

Some manufacturers came to PVC as one product among many. Sarnafil built its reputation specifically around PVC membrane, which is worth knowing when a bid or spec references this name specifically rather than a generalist single-ply manufacturer.

Sika Sarnafil's Background

Sarnafil is a PVC roofing membrane product line with a long operating history that predates much of the current single-ply membrane market, now owned by Sika, a global building materials company headquartered in Switzerland. Because Sarnafil's product development has centered on PVC membrane specifically for decades rather than treating it as a secondary product line, their catalog reflects a depth of PVC-specific formulation work that's a genuine differentiator among membrane manufacturers.

Core PVC Membrane Products

Sarnafil's flagship membrane products cover standard commercial mil thicknesses for heat-welded PVC roofing, used in the same chemical and grease-resistant applications as any manufacturer's PVC line: rooftop kitchen exhaust, food processing exposure, and industrial chemical environments where TPO or EPDM would degrade prematurely. As with all PVC regardless of manufacturer, seam quality at install, meaning weld temperature and probing discipline, is the primary determinant of long-term seam performance. Impact resistance under hail follows the same rule as any welded membrane: thickness and reinforcement drive puncture resistance, not the brand name on the roll, which is why we still walk through mil thickness selection against the building's actual exposure rather than treating a recognizable manufacturer as a substitute for that conversation.

Reinforced and Fleece-Backed Options

Sarnafil offers reinforced membrane variants intended for use in fully-adhered assemblies over rigid insulation, similar in concept to fleece-backed products from other manufacturers, providing additional puncture resistance from the underside of the membrane. This is a relevant option on recover projects where an existing roof surface has some irregularity that a standard mechanically-attached sheet wouldn't accommodate as cleanly. On older buildings around this market where an existing built-up or modified bitumen roof has settled unevenly over the years, a fleece-backed sheet can bridge minor surface irregularities that would otherwise telegraph through a thinner, unreinforced membrane.

  • Heat-welded PVC membrane in standard commercial mil thicknesses
  • Reinforced and fleece-backed PVC variants for fully-adhered assemblies
  • Wide color range within their PVC line, beyond the standard white and gray options
  • Chemical-resistant formulation options for specific industrial exposure conditions
  • Accessory and flashing membrane matched to field sheet chemical resistance grade

Why PVC Specialization Matters on the Right Building

On a building with rooftop kitchen exhaust, chemical storage venting, or industrial process exposure, the actual chemical resistance formulation of the PVC sheet matters considerably more than it does on a standard office or retail roof. A manufacturer whose core business has been PVC-specific formulation for a long operating history is a reasonable place to look when that specific exposure profile is driving the membrane decision, though the same seam-quality and installation caveats that apply to any PVC product still apply here.

Color and Aesthetic Options

Sarnafil's PVC line is also known for offering a broader color range than the standard white or gray typical of most commercial membrane, which occasionally matters on buildings with visible rooftop areas from adjacent structures or where an owner has a specific aesthetic requirement. This is a secondary consideration relative to performance specification, but worth mentioning since it does distinguish this product line from most competitors. We still evaluate thickness and reinforcement first on any project where color is part of the conversation, since an appealing color option on an under-specified membrane doesn't change what the roof needs to survive a Missouri hailstorm, and we'd rather an owner pick color last, after the performance spec is locked in, than the other way around.

Reading a Sarnafil-Specified Bid Correctly

A proposal naming Sarnafil should still specify the exact product line, mil thickness, and whether a standard or reinforced membrane is being proposed, the same due diligence that applies to any manufacturer-named bid. We include that detail explicitly rather than letting a recognizable manufacturer name substitute for actual specification clarity. A well-known brand on a proposal isn't a substitute for a scope breakdown, and we've seen bids elsewhere in this market lean on manufacturer reputation to skip over exactly the details, thickness, attachment method, flashing spec, that determine how the roof actually performs.

Warranty Structure

As with other major manufacturers, Sarnafil's warranty coverage is generally tied to using matched, approved system components installed per their published specifications, which is worth confirming directly for any project where warranty terms factor into the decision.

Questions on Sika Sarnafil Products

Is Sarnafil PVC different from other manufacturers' PVC membrane?

The core chemistry category is similar, but Sarnafil's long specialization in PVC-specific formulation is a relevant factor for buildings with demanding chemical exposure requirements.

Does Sarnafil PVC cost more than a generalist manufacturer's PVC line?

Pricing varies by market conditions and specific product selected, so this should be compared directly on the specific project rather than assumed.

Is reinforced Sarnafil PVC necessary for a standard recover project?

Not always. It depends on the existing roof surface condition and whether additional underside puncture resistance is warranted for that specific substrate.

Can Sarnafil PVC be specified in colors other than white?

Yes, their product line includes a broader color range than most standard commercial membrane offerings.

Does PVC from any manufacturer resist Springfield hail as well as thicker TPO?

Impact resistance depends primarily on membrane thickness within the specific product line selected, which we size to the building's actual hail exposure rather than a default gauge.
